title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for http api resources, representations, errors, and handler layers and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for consistent feed-reader HTTP API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Separate routing, authority, request admission, application command, repository, representation, and observability with narrow interfaces.
Apply safe and idempotent method semantics, success statuses, preconditions, redirects, and retry guidance consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Resource Model · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Resource Model
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 7273 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Run the Feed Go Request Cancellation boundary case for consistent feed-reader HTTP API, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Feed Go Request Cancellation result because visible consistent feed-reader HTTP API output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Feed Go Request Cancellation.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Request Cancellation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Request Cancellation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 9606 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Propagate the request context through application, database, and downstream work while preventing client disconnect from corrupting durable jobs.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Request Cancellation.
steps.why: Retrieving Feed Go Request Cancellation through the concrete consistent feed-reader HTTP API case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Request Cancellation, keep Feed Go Request Cancellation connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Propagate the request context through application, database, and downstream work while preventing client disconnect from corrupting durable jobs.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. For Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Request Cancellation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every disconnect should cancel already-committed ingestion work.
